response.justification: The candidate is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. The most critical disqualifier is C++ experience: the role mandates 8 years, but the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis side project — C++ does not even appear in the skills section. The candidate's background is overwhelmingly web and backend (TypeScript, Node.js, D-lang), with no evidence of cloud compute platform ownership, large-scale infrastructure architecture, or technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineering teams.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, pair programming, trunk-based development, CI/CD) practiced consistently across 10+ years of roles
Some large-scale distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, high-performance requirements
Active community contributor through public talks, workshops, and open-source projects, demonstrating communication skills and a mentoring disposition
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — estimated under one year versus the eight-year hard minimum; the language is absent from the candidate's own skills summary
No cloud compute platform ownership or infrastructure architecture; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is application-level tooling, not platform or compute infrastructure design

No demonstrated technical leadership of infrastructure or cloud engineering teams; staff and senior titles across the resume are individual-contributor-oriented with no direct reports or platform-scope ownership cited

key_factors.factor: No cloud platform or compute infrastructure ownership
Established software craftsmanship and testing practices
